title: System Response to Internal Conditions: The Zero-Input Response
summary: The page explains how total response is decomposed into zero-input and zero-state components and clarifies the distinction between initial conditions at t = 0− and t = 0+ for the total response versus the zero-input response. It then begins Example 2.4, which sets up an RLC circuit problem to determine the zero-input response from given initial inductor current and capacitor voltage conditions.
